name: ExtensionAppointmentResponse_RecurrenceId
title: R5: The recurrence ID of the specific recurring request (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `AppointmentResponse.recurrenceId` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`AppointmentResponse.recurrenceId` 0..1 `positiveInt`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `AppointmentResponse.recurrenceId` 0..1 `positiveInt`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `AppointmentResponse.recurrenceId` has a context of AppointmentResponse based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AppointmentResponse`.
Element `AppointmentResponse.recurrenceId` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The recurrence ID of the specific recurring request (new)
element.comment:
Element `AppointmentResponse.recurrenceId` has a context of AppointmentResponse based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AppointmentResponse`.
Element `AppointmentResponse.recurrenceId` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If the recurrence template was defined using the list of occurrenceDates then this property might not be used.

element.short: The recurrence ID of the specific recurring request
element.definition: The recurrence ID (sequence number) of the specific appointment when responding to a recurring request.
element.comment: If the recurrence template was defined using the list of occurrenceDates then this property might not be used.
